skill.name: azure-search-documents-dotnet
skill.description: Guidance for building Azure AI Search applications with the .NET Azure.Search.Documents SDK, including index management, document operations, full-text search, vector search, semantic search, and hybrid search.
skill.summary: Build .NET search applications with Azure.Search.Documents patterns for indexes, documents, and relevance features.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Manual review dismissed the high static score as Markdown and SDK documentation false positives. The skill contains Azure endpoint and API key examples, but they are user supplied configuration patterns with no evidence of exfiltration, obfuscation, prompt injection, or automatic execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static command-execution alerts are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline code, and documentation examples. The skill does not include scripts or instructions that execute commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are within Markdown documentation for Bash or C# examples. No executable skill script, hook, or hidden automation is present.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Azure Endpoint Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL alert points to a placeholder Azure Search endpoint format. It is not an external collection endpoint or exfiltration target.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The value uses an angle-bracket placeholder for the user service name. It documents normal Azure SDK configuration, not a live destination.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Examples Handle User Credentials
low_findings.description: The environment variable alerts are legitimate documentation for reading a user supplied Azure Search API key. The skill recommends DefaultAzureCredential for production and does not transmit the key outside Azure SDK usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key appears only in configuration examples. Nearby text states API key authentication is not recommended for production.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Terminology Matches
low_findings.description: The cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from search SDK terminology, analyzers, vectors, sorting, and reference links. No cryptographic implementation or system discovery behavior is described.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found Azure Search concepts at these locations. I found no encryption code, host probing, or system inventory instructions.

low_findings.title: Aggregate Dangerous-Combination Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combines false-positive command alerts with normal Azure endpoint and credential examples. Manual review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, obfuscation, or data exfiltration pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious combination is explained by documentation snippets for installing and using the Azure SDK. No hidden execution path was found.

content.user_title: Build Azure AI Search Apps in .NET
content.value_statement: Building search features with Azure.Search.Documents can require many SDK patterns. This skill gives focused .NET guidance for indexes, documents, vectors, semantic ranking, and hybrid search.
actual_capabilities: Shows package installation for Azure.Search.Documents and Azure.Identity.
Explains when to use SearchClient, SearchIndexClient, and SearchIndexerClient.
Provides index creation patterns with FieldBuilder and manual field definitions.
Covers document upload, merge, upsert, delete, and batch operations.
Demonstrates full-text, faceted, autocomplete, vector, semantic, and hybrid search patterns.
Includes basic error handling and production-oriented SDK best practices.
limitations: Does not provision Azure resources or manage cloud infrastructure.
Version details may become outdated as the Azure SDK changes.
Examples assume an existing Azure AI Search service and index data.
Does not provide deep coverage for non-.NET Azure Search SDKs.

use_cases.title: Add Search to a .NET App
use_cases.description: Use the SDK examples to create clients, query indexes, and return selected fields.

use_cases.title: Design an Azure Search Index
use_cases.description: Model fields, filters, facets, vectors, and semantic settings before creating or updating an index.

use_cases.title: Improve Search Relevance
use_cases.description: Combine keyword, vector, and semantic search patterns for stronger result ranking.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Search Client
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new .NET search feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Azure.Search.Documents in a .NET app using DefaultAzureCredential and a SearchClient.

prompt_templates.title: Define an Index Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate index planning for typed C# models.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an Azure AI Search index for my C# model with filters, facets, sortable fields, and a key field.

prompt_templates.title: Add Vector and Semantic Search
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced relevance features for natural language search.
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how to add vector search and semantic ranking to an existing Azure.Search.Documents query flow.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Search Implementation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced code review for reliability and relevance.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Azure.Search.Documents usage for client selection, index design, batching, filters, and error handling.

output_examples.input: Ask for a .NET setup path for Azure AI Search.
output: Client selection guidance for querying and index management.
Configuration notes for endpoint, index name, and credentials.

output_examples.input: Ask how to build a hotel search index.
output: Field plan with key, searchable, filterable, sortable, and facetable fields.
Vector field guidance for embedding-based retrieval.
Index creation steps using the .NET SDK.

output_examples.input: Ask how to combine keyword, vector, and semantic search.
output: Semantic configuration notes for captions and answers.
Result handling guidance for scores, captions, and selected fields.

best_practices: Prefer DefaultAzureCredential over API keys for production workloads.
Use FieldBuilder and attributes when typed models fit the index design.
Return only required fields with Select to reduce payload size.
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode Azure Search API keys in source files.
Do not return every field when the application only displays a subset.
Do not mix vector and semantic search without a clear relevance goal.

faq.question: What Azure SDK package does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers Azure.Search.Documents for .NET, with Azure.Identity for preferred authentication.

faq.question: Does it create Azure resources for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides SDK guidance and assumes you already have an Azure AI Search service.

faq.question: Can it help with vector search?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes vector field setup and query patterns for embedding-based search.

faq.question: Can it help with semantic search?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains semantic query options, captions, answers, and hybrid search patterns.

faq.question: Is API key authentication recommended?
faq.answer: The skill shows API key usage, but recommends DefaultAzureCredential for production.

faq.question: Does it support indexer and skillset guidance?
faq.answer: It identifies SearchIndexerClient for indexers, skillsets, and data sources, but detailed examples are limited.
